Good: Air quality is currently good and is expected to remain so over the next 6 hours. No action required, but monitoring continues.
Satisfactory: Air quality is satisfactory at your campus. Sensitive individuals like asthmatic students should still monitor symptoms as a precaution.
Moderate: Predicted AQI will reach Moderate levels within 6 hours — pollutants are accumulating due to stagnant air. Consider postponing outdoor sports and keeping windows closed, especially in hostel and library blocks.
Poor: Air quality is predicted to reach Poor levels in 6 hours, driven by low wind speed and a trapped boundary layer below 500m. Avoid all outdoor activities and issue an advisory to students to stay indoors.
Very Poor: AQI is predicted to spike to Very Poor levels within 6 hours — trapped pollutants and low winds are concentrating PM2.5 near ground level. Immediately cancel all outdoor events and activate indoor air purifiers in common areas.
Severe: A severe AQI spike is expected in 6 hours. Pollutants are critically trapped near the ground. Issue an emergency advisory, cancel all outdoor activities, and ensure N95 masks are available for staff on duty.
Hazardous: Hazardous air quality is predicted to hit campus in 6 hours. This is a serious health emergency level. Declare a campus air quality alert, suspend all outdoor operations, and ensure medical support is on standby.
